Overview

Address
6810 South Hazel Street, Pine Bluff, AR 71603
Phone
8705410342
Certified beds
177
Avg daily residents
71 (40% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2003-03-19
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Resident count too small for reliable rating.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-10-14 39 30 18 462 2 693
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-12-01 31 20 13 288 1 288
Total weighted health score
591.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (85)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

83 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2026-01-22 —
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-06-16 2025-07-07
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2025-06-16 2025-07-07
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-06-16 2025-07-07
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2025-04-18 2025-05-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2025-04-18 2025-05-18
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2025-04-18 2025-05-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2025-04-18 2025-05-18
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2025-04-18 2025-05-18
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-03
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-10-14 2025-01-02
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. D 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. D 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2024-10-14 2025-01-02
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-10-14 2025-01-02
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-02
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2024-10-14 2025-01-02
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-10-14 2025-01-02
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. F 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2024-10-14 2024-11-04
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-02
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-03
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-03
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. E 2024-10-14 2025-01-02
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-03
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-10-14 2025-01-02
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-10-14 2024-11-26
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-10-14 2024-12-04
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2024-07-02 2024-07-31
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2024-07-02 2024-07-31
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. E 2024-07-02 2024-07-31
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2024-07-02 2024-07-31
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-05-24 2024-06-24
0926 Have policies on smoking. D 2024-04-04 2024-05-04
0567 Honor the resident's right to manage his or her financial affairs. F 2023-12-01 2024-01-01
0569 Notify each resident of certain balances and convey resident funds upon discharge, eviction, or death. F 2023-12-01 2024-01-01
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2023-12-01 2024-01-01

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2024, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
